MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-08-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Virginia A. Crisp, Esq., 415-772-5771, appeared on behalf of defendant and moving party Stewart Morton. Edward P. Kelly, Esq., 415-920-9123, appeared on behalf of plaintiff and opposing party Janet Mosqueda.

After argument the tentative ruling was adopted as the order of the Court: Stewart Morton's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted No triable issue of fact exists re defendants retained control over the operative details of plaintiffs work or ownership/control over the property on which the injury occurred.

Prevailing party to prepare orders pursuant to 437 c(g). (MD)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-08-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael K. Tcheng, Esq., 415-391-6182, appeared on behalf of defendants and moving parties Desmond-LeGrand Properties et al. Edward P. Kelly, Esq., 415-920-9123, appeared on behalf of plaintiff and opposing party Janet Mosqueda.

After argument the tentative ruling was adopted as the order of the Court: Desmond-Legrand's Motion for Summary Judgment which is joined by Vanguard Properties. Summary judgment is denied. Regarding :negligence cause of action failure to bear burden of proof regarding the issue of duty. Regarding premises liability there is a triable issue of fact exists. Regarding defendant's control of premises where injury occurred. Re loss consortium, this being derivative, is also denied.

Prevailing party to prepare orders pursuant to 437 c(g). (MD) Hon. James J. McBride. Reporter Judy Thomsen, CSR 5591
